scenario: A team writes DMN decisions and wants to unit test them with various input combinations to verify correctness. They wonder about tooling.
question: What tools support DMN testing?

options.text: Claiming there are no tools for testing DMN decisions in Camunda 8 does not reflect the actual behavior. Multiple testing mechanisms exist: Web Modeler Play mode allows interactive process execution including DMN evaluation within BPMN flows; the EvaluateDecision REST API (POST /v2/decision-definitions/evaluation) allows scripted, process-independent decision invocation ideal for CI pipelines; Camunda Process Test (CPT) provides a JUnit-style harness for end-to-end process and DMN testing; and the Operate Decision Instance view lets teams inspect historical rule matches and outputs. The ecosystem provides options across the full testing spectrum from interactive exploration to automated regression suites.

8. Web Modeler Play mode allows interactive process execution including DMN evaluation within BPMN flows. The EvaluateDecision REST API endpoint (POST /v2/decision-definitions/evaluation) enables scripted, process-independent invocation of DMN decisions, making it ideal for CI pipeline integration. Camunda Process Test (CPT) provides a JUnit-style harness for automated end-to-end testing of both processes and embedded DMN. The Operate Decision Instance view lets teams inspect historical rule matches. These options cover interactive, scripted, and automated testing scenarios.

The EvaluateDecision REST API provides a scriptable, process-independent endpoint that can be called from any HTTP client or CI pipeline without human interaction. Camunda Process Test (CPT) embeds a Camunda 8 runtime in-process for JUnit-style automated tests that assert BRT outcomes. These automated approaches enable regression testing of DMN decisions as part of a normal development workflow, with the same rigor applied to code unit tests. Fully automated DMN testing without any manual steps is achievable and recommended.

Claiming that every DMN test must start a full BPMN process instance is only partially accurate. While end-to-end tests via Web Modeler Play or Camunda Process Test do involve process instances, the EvaluateDecision REST API (POST /v2/decision-definitions/evaluation) allows direct, process-independent decision invocation. This approach evaluates the DMN decision in isolation, without spawning any BPMN process instance, making it significantly faster and simpler for unit-style tests targeting specific decision logic.

a.text: Incorrect. DMN testing tooling spans interactive and automated (note: no "DMN Tester" feature exists inside the Web Modeler DMN editor in 8.8 -- that's a fabricated name; Play and EvaluateDecision are the actual paths): Interactive (development-time) -- Web Modeler Play mode (per play-your-process.md): Click the Play tab on a BPMN diagram that includes a BRT referencing your decision. Play deploys the BPMN and all linked DMN files to a development cluster (:21). Start the process with example data; Play executes the entire process including DMN tables, FEEL, forms, connectors (:72). Inspect Instance History + Variables; see which rules fired in Operate. SaaS only; in Self-Managed Play uses a configured cluster (:17). EvaluateDecision REST endpoint (Orchestration Cluster API, per business-rule-tasks.md:19-22): POST /v2/decision-definitions/evaluation with decisionDefinitionKey or decisionDefinitionId + variables. Returns decision result independently of any process -- pure DMN evaluation. Scriptable from any HTTP client; ideal for CI test suites. Also exposed via Zeebe gRPC EvaluateDecision RPC for SDKs. End-to-end testing -- Camunda Process Test (CPT) / spring-boot-starter testing module: JUnit-style harness embeds a Camunda 8 runtime in-process. Deploy BPMN+DMN; create instance; assert BRT result. Doc: apis-tools/testing/ (Spring Boot starter + plain Java testing). Operate Decision Instance view: Browse historical evaluations; inspect inputs/outputs/rule matches. Useful for production debugging, not for pre-deployment unit tests. Test strategy for DMN-heavy systems: Unit-style (fast, in CI): EvaluateDecision-API tests per decision. Coverage: design tests covering each rule + edge cases (boundary values, no-match scenarios). Regression: lock down current behaviour; alert on changes. Build a test culture for DMN -- same rigour as BPMN / code tests.
